seoTitle: Bonshaw vs Bony Mountain — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Bonshaw vs Bony Mountain: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bonshaw and Bony Mountain.
heading: Bonshaw vs Bony Mountain — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bonshaw, Queensland and Bony Mountain, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bony Mountain has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Bonshaw (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bonshaw or Bony Mountain safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bonshaw has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Bony Mountain scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bonshaw scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Bony Mountain scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bonshaw or Bony Mountain?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bony Mountain has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
